outputs.project_binding: Synthetic public-safe calibration binding for knowledge_access_event_capture_v0. Scope is limited to metadata-only access events for existing knowledge references; no source payloads, private packets, runtime paths, or advisory text are included.
outputs.knowledge_access_event_batch: Batch contains three synthetic metadata-only access events: event_001 references node_ref:workflow_alpha during task_ref:task_capture; event_002 references node_ref:skill_beta during task_ref:task_capture; event_003 references node_ref:packet_gamma during handoff_ref:handoff_review. Events carry only stable refs, event ids, access category, timestamp bucket, and outcome state.
outputs.normalized_access_event_log: Normalized log preserves event_id, stable_node_ref, optional edge_ref, access_context_ref, access_type, timestamp_bucket, outcome_state, and confidence_hint. All three rows are treated as metadata-only ledger/register rows and not as source truth.
outputs.usage_rollup: Rollup reports three total synthetic access events across three referenced nodes. node_ref:workflow_alpha receives the highest recency-weighted count and is the only hot retention candidate. node_ref:skill_beta and node_ref:packet_gamma remain unpromoted candidate observations.
outputs.retention_label_packet: Retention packet assigns candidate label hot to node_ref:workflow_alpha based on synthetic recency/count policy. No archive, stale, or retire action is accepted. Label is a candidate signal pending project policy or owner decision.
outputs.link_strength_analysis: Analysis identifies one orphan candidate edge: edge_ref:skill_beta_to_packet_gamma has observed adjacency but insufficient confirmed relation support. It is marked weak/orphan_candidate with low confidence and no canon promotion.
outputs.knowledge_accumulation_delta: Delta records metadata-only observed changes: three new access observations, one hot retention candidate, one orphan candidate edge, and one graph update candidate. Delta is not ontology acceptance, source truth, or canon promotion.
outputs.graph_update_packet: Graph update candidate contains node refs node_ref:workflow_alpha, node_ref:skill_beta, node_ref:packet_gamma; event refs event_001, event_002, event_003; candidate edge edge_ref:skill_beta_to_packet_gamma; counts, recency bucket, outcome states, and confidence hints. It is suitable only for later graph or visualization review.
outputs.orphan_redundancy_candidate_register: Register contains one orphan candidate: edge_ref:skill_beta_to_packet_gamma, reason: observed metadata adjacency without enough confirmed relation evidence. No redundant node candidate is asserted.
outputs.boundary_review_note: Public-safe synthetic output only. It does not include copied source text, private packet payloads, advisory answers, credentials, local runtime paths, hidden refs, tool traces, file edits, or upstream mutations.

provenance: Public-safe synthetic workflow contract supplied in prompt
Synthetic scenario facts supplied in prompt: three metadata-only access events, one hot retention label, one orphan candidate edge, one graph update packet candidate
gaps: No real ledger/register rows were provided or inspected
Retention and link labels remain candidate signals because no project policy acceptance or owner decision is included
downstream_handoff: Route retention_label_packet to a later retention policy review before any action
Route graph_update_packet and orphan_redundancy_candidate_register to later graph/visualization or ontology review without canon promotion
boundary_review_note: No upstream artifact mutation, runtime path disclosure, private evidence claim, or source truth promotion is made
no_claims: No claim of tool use, file reads, file edits, runtime execution, hidden reference access, or private/raw evidence inspection
No claim that draft workflow output is production-ready, canon authority, ontology acceptance, or final retention action
